title: Implement Device Studio hub websocket client
description:
Implement the client-side adapter that lets Device Studio connect to the existing realtime hub websocket as a simulated satellite. It should send hello with device/profile capabilities, support user.text turns, interrupt, ping/pong, and consume hub message/audio/text lifecycle events.

It must support both live hub mode and mock mode. Mock mode should exercise the UI and behavior system without requiring Deepgram, ElevenLabs, PSFN, Hermes, or physical hardware. The adapter should preserve the existing hub protocol and only introduce new messages behind a documented extension if necessary.
acceptance_criteria: Device Studio can connect to a running hub or mock transport. It receives assistant/user message events, reflects connection state, sends typed turns and interrupts, logs all structured events, and has tests for handshake/message parsing behavior. Existing hub tests continue to pass.

title: Implement behavior library, import/export, and playback engine
description:
Build the behavior subsystem for loading behavior definitions, listing them by profile/channel/provenance, importing/exporting JSON, and playing frames through time. It should support movement + expression timelines, expression-only one-frame behaviors, source labels, generated-vs-official provenance, and hardware verification state.

The playback engine should emit normalized render state at a given timestamp so browser previews, tests, and future firmware exporters can share the same interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: Behavior fixtures load into a library, can be imported/exported as JSON, and can be played deterministically. Tests cover duration, frame ordering, sampling/interpolation policy, profile/channel filtering, and event emission for frame apply/playback start/stop.

title: Add Device Studio app scaffold and npm scripts
description:
Add a repo-local Device Studio application scaffold suitable for browser-based simulation and authoring. It should be clearly separated from core hub code, with scripts to run and build it. Keep dependency choices conservative and compatible with the existing TypeScript/npm setup.

The first screen should be the actual studio surface, not a marketing page: preview area, profile selector, live/mock status, behavior/timeline area, and command/event log placeholders.
acceptance_criteria: A documented npm script launches Device Studio locally. The scaffold builds under the repo TypeScript/tooling conventions. The initial UI has stable regions for preview, controls, behavior list, and event log without changing production hub startup.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio app scaffold on agents/device-studio-app

title: Add Stack-chan and Waveshare ESP32 device profiles
description:
Add concrete profile data for the first two target device classes. Stack-chan should model screen, face, touch, speaker/mic, RGB LED, and two servo movement channels where supported by the reference hardware. The Waveshare ESP32-S3 1.85 inch round LCD board should model a 360x360 round screen, touch capability where present, and screen-centric output capabilities.

Profiles should include source/provenance notes and hardware verification status. Avoid pretending untested movement ranges are hardware-safe; mark them as unverified until measured on a real unit.
acceptance_criteria: Profile fixtures exist for Stack-chan and Waveshare round LCD. Tests or validation checks confirm profiles load, expose capabilities correctly, and degrade behavior channels appropriately. The data includes source/provenance and verification fields.

title: Device Studio architecture and protocol contract
description:
Write a self-contained architecture document for the ESP32 companion Device Studio. It should explain why this is a behavioral simulator rather than a CPU/peripheral emulator, what belongs in tooling versus core hub runtime, how live/mock modes work, and how the tool talks to the existing realtime websocket hub.

The contract must cover device profiles, behavior timelines, expression/viseme state, movement channels, structured command/event logging, provenance, and hardware verification states. It should explicitly call out compatibility with Stack-chan-style servo/screen devices and screen-only ESP32 devices like the Waveshare round board.
acceptance_criteria: A docs page exists and describes the tool boundaries, live/mock flows, data model concepts, websocket message usage, protocol extension candidates, testing strategy, and MVP/non-goals. It must be detailed enough that implementation workers do not need to consult the original chat screenshot or conversation.
close_reason: Completed Device Studio architecture/protocol contract on agents/device-studio-spec

title: Define device profile and behavior timeline model
description:
Create TypeScript types and validation helpers for Device Studio profiles and behavior timelines. The model should represent screen dimensions/shape, touch capability, input/output capabilities, movement joints, expression/viseme channels, LEDs/backlight, provenance, hardware verification status, and timeline frames.

The model must be device-neutral: the same semantic behavior should be renderable on Stack-chan with servo motion and on a smaller LCD-only ESP32 by ignoring unsupported channels without failing. Include fixtures for representative behavior data such as neutral, happy/laughing, angry, and a short dance/sing-along style timeline.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript model code and focused tests exist. Tests cover required fields, degradation on unsupported channels, timeline ordering/duration calculation, provenance labels, and hardware verification state. The code is reusable by both CLI/headless tools and browser UI.
close_reason: Completed shared Device Studio profile/behavior model on agents/device-studio-model

title: Build ESP32 companion Device Studio and simulator tooling
description:
Build repo-local tooling for ESP32-class embodied companion devices. The goal is a Device Studio that can connect to the existing PSFN Satellite Hub as a simulated satellite, preview device-specific screens/motion, author behavior timelines, and export assets/behavior data for later firmware work. This is intentionally tooling, not core hub runtime: it should live outside the production transport path while reusing the existing realtime websocket protocol and satellite capability vocabulary.

Targets include Stack-chan-style devices with screen, face, servo movement, LEDs, and app-server-controlled behaviors, plus smaller/lower-power ESP32 screens such as the Waveshare ESP32-S3 1.85 inch round LCD board. The authoring model should let one semantic behavior degrade cleanly across devices: Stack-chan can use movement + expression + viseme; a round LCD may use only screen/expression/viseme/touch; larger clients can later map the same semantic state to Three.js.

Important product shape: mirror the Stack-chan simulator screenshot pattern: profile selector, live/mock backend mode, command/event log, behavior library, timeline/frame playback, provenance labels such as official/device-derived vs host-generated, and a hardware verification state so host-generated movement is not treated as proven until tested on a real unit.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is complete when Device Studio can be launched from this repo, connect to a local hub or mock backend, simulate at least Stack-chan and Waveshare round LCD profiles, play/import/export behavior timelines, render expression/viseme state, log structured commands/events, and document the hardware verification workflow. Production hub behavior must remain unchanged unless a deliberately documented protocol extension is added with tests.
notes:
Execution plan created 2026-05-11:

Layer 0 can run in parallel:
- psfn-bsy.1 architecture/protocol contract, assigned to worktree agents/device-studio-spec.
- psfn-bsy.2 shared profile/behavior model and tests, assigned to worktree agents/device-studio-model.
- psfn-bsy.3 browser Device Studio scaffold and npm scripts, assigned to worktree agents/device-studio-app.

Layer 1 unlocks after model/scaffold:
- psfn-bsy.4 concrete Stack-chan and Waveshare profiles depends on psfn-bsy.2.
- psfn-bsy.5 hub websocket client depends on psfn-bsy.2 and psfn-bsy.3.
- psfn-bsy.6 behavior library/playback/import-export depends on psfn-bsy.2 and psfn-bsy.3.

Layer 2+ then builds renderers, authoring, command log, protocol extension tests, exports, docs, and final QA. Keep generated/unverified behavior distinct from hardware-verified behavior throughout.

title: Validate Voxta facade against AcidBubbles plugin
description: Run the new SignalR-compatible facade against the actual VaM/AcidBubbles Voxta plugin, capture any protocol mismatches, and add compatibility fixtures for the observed framing.

title: Add Voxta-compatible satellite routes
description: Implement the first Voxta-compatible facade for VaM/AcidBubbles-style plugin integration: SignalR-like hub route, message framing, capability/auth handshake, and routing into the existing PSFN embodied session seam.
close_reason: Implemented initial Voxta-compatible SignalR facade and route wiring

title: Add Hermes API-server runtime adapter
description: Use the latest hermes-agent OpenAI-compatible API server as the hub-facing Hermes runtime path, preserving stable hub session ids without relying on private in-process Hermes adapter internals.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes API-server runtime adapter with runtime selection and tests

title: Add PSFN embodied channel seam
description: Introduce the initial hub channel boundary for PSFN integration so the thin shell/test surface can exercise PSFN-backed turns through a stable channel identity.
close_reason: Implemented initial PSFN embodied channel seam, typed thin-shell turn path, and tests

title: Device Studio end-to-end QA and integration polish
description:
Final integration pass for the Device Studio epic. Run quality gates, smoke the live/mock flows, confirm the studio is visually usable across desktop/mobile-ish widths, verify behavior import/export, and ensure production hub behavior remains stable.

This is also the point to reconcile outputs from parallel worktrees, resolve naming inconsistencies, and update beads with any discovered follow-up work.
acceptance_criteria: All Device Studio beads are complete or have explicit follow-up beads. npm checks/tests pass or documented blockers exist. The studio launches, mock mode works without credentials, live mode can connect to the hub, docs are current, and git status is clean after commit/push.

title: Add Device Studio unit and integration test coverage
description:
Add focused tests for the Device Studio tooling: model validation, profile loading, behavior playback, import/export, mock/live transport message handling, and any protocol extensions. Include useful logging around simulated hub events and behavior playback so failures are easy to diagnose.

Keep tests compatible with the existing npm test/check flow and avoid requiring physical hardware or paid provider credentials.
acceptance_criteria: npm run check:ts and npm run test:ts cover the shared Device Studio model/transport logic. Any browser-only checks have a documented command and do not require hardware. Tests fail clearly on malformed profiles, invalid behavior frames, and websocket message regressions.

title: Document Device Studio usage and hardware verification workflow
description:
Write the operator documentation for running Device Studio, selecting profiles, connecting live/mock transports, importing/exporting behaviors, and moving a behavior from host-generated to hardware-verified. Include guidance for Stack-chan intake and Waveshare round LCD intake.

The workflow should prevent accidental confusion between simulator-only movement and hardware-tested movement.
acceptance_criteria: README or docs updates include launch commands, profile descriptions, live/mock setup, behavior import/export examples, and a hardware verification checklist. Documentation calls out what is simulator-only versus tested on real devices.

title: Document and test embodiment protocol extensions
description:
If Device Studio needs structured embodiment messages beyond the existing realtime protocol, define them explicitly and add tests. Candidate messages include embodiment.state or behavior.command events carrying expression, viseme, gaze, servo, LED, and display state.

Do not silently overload existing text/audio events with simulator-only meaning. Existing realtime clients must continue working. Any new message shape should be optional, capability-gated, and documented.
acceptance_criteria: Protocol docs and TypeScript types/tests exist for any added message. Existing protocol tests pass. If no extension is needed for the MVP, this bead should close with a note explaining why the current protocol plus local simulator state was sufficient.

title: Add live/mock app-server panel and structured command event log
description:
Implement the right-side operational panel inspired by the Stack-chan simulator screenshot: live/mock mode, connection status, send controls, generated draft/import actions, and an append-only command/event log. The log should show structured JSON events such as behavior frame application, behavior import/export, hub message receipt, interrupt, and hardware verification changes.

This panel should make it obvious whether events came from live hub traffic, mock traffic, user editing, or host-side generation.
acceptance_criteria: The studio exposes live/mock state, logs structured events with timestamps/source/profile/session where applicable, and supports sending a typed user turn through the selected transport. Event log output is testable and exportable or copyable for debugging.

title: Add behavior authoring controls for joints, expressions, visemes, and timelines
description:
Add the editor controls needed to build or tweak behaviors inside Device Studio: select profile, choose expression/viseme, adjust joint values, add/update/delete timeline frames, duplicate behaviors, and mark generated behaviors as pending hardware verification.

Controls should be practical for repeated engineering use: sliders or numeric fields for joints, segmented/toggle controls for modes, profile-aware enabled/disabled states, and clear command buttons for import/export/play/duplicate/delete. The first screen should remain the studio workspace.
acceptance_criteria: A user can create a new behavior, edit frames, preview playback, duplicate existing behaviors, export JSON, and see unsupported controls disabled by selected profile. Generated behaviors retain provenance and hardware verification status.

title: Render 2D LCD avatar and touch simulation
description:
Implement a profile-aware 2D display preview for ESP32 screen devices. It should render square and round displays at native aspect/shape, show expression/viseme states, and simulate touch events on profiles that support touch. This is the primary path for smaller LCD-only devices such as the Waveshare round board.

Use stable dimensions and responsive constraints so the preview does not resize unpredictably as states change. The renderer should be driven by the shared behavior playback state, not hardcoded UI-only state.
acceptance_criteria: The studio renders Stack-chan and Waveshare screen previews with expression/viseme changes, round-screen clipping for the Waveshare profile, and touch event logging. Tests or deterministic render-state checks cover profile-aware display sizing and channel degradation.

title: Render Stack-chan 3D preview and joint visualization
description:
Implement the Stack-chan movement preview surface. It should visualize the body/screen and pan/tilt joint channels so authored behaviors can be inspected before hardware flashing. Use Three.js or a similarly appropriate 3D renderer if adding a web app renderer; keep the model lightweight and avoid blocking the MVP on perfect CAD fidelity.

The preview must make unverified hardware status visible and must not claim motion ranges are safe until real hardware intake confirms them.
acceptance_criteria: A Stack-chan preview responds to behavior playback frames and joint state. The preview can run in mock mode, has nonblank render verification where practical, and clearly marks hardware-unverified behavior. It does not affect core hub runtime.

title: Lowercase repository name and local path references
description: Rename the GitHub repository and local checkout path to lowercase, then update README absolute links to match the lowercase checkout path.
close_reason: Renamed GitHub repo and local checkout to lowercase; updated README path links

title: Update README for Voxta facade
description: Document the newly added Voxta-compatible hub facade, including supported routes, message coverage, config, limitations, and follow-up work.
close_reason: README updated with Voxta facade usage, configuration, limitations, and architecture notes

title: Add Voxta URL audio playback artifacts
description: Extend the Voxta facade to produce playback-ready local audio artifacts for VaM/Voxta clients, expose them over a hub route, and emit the Voxta speech/audio metadata expected by the AcidBubbles plugin.

title: Add Voxta vision upload handling
description: Implement the Voxta vision capture/upload path for VaM/Voxta clients and route posted images into the embodied session metadata/tooling surface without bypassing PSFN policy.

title: Add sprite and firmware asset export pipeline
description:
Add export tooling for assets that future ESP32 firmware can consume. The first target is small-screen avatar assets: PNG sprite sheets and/or generated RGB565-ready data for expressions/visemes, plus behavior JSON that can be embedded or served.

This should not require firmware integration yet. It should produce deterministic artifacts from the same behavior/profile model used by the studio so the emulator and firmware assets do not diverge.
acceptance_criteria: Export commands or UI actions can generate deterministic sprite/behavior artifacts for at least one profile. Outputs are documented, ignored or stored intentionally, and covered by golden or structural tests where appropriate.
